name: Zhou Yu (Gongjin)
page_title: Zhou Yu (Gongjin) 周瑜 (公瑾) [Yuan Shu, Wu] – Romance of the Three Kingdoms Encyclopedia – Kongming’s Archives
novel_notes:
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
	
		
	Zhou Yu did not attempt to have Zhuge Liang assassinated. The rivalry between Zhou Yu and Zhuge Liang was added to add to the allure of Zhuge Liang.
	Was not visited by Jiang Gan during the battle of Chibi.
	Zhuge Liang did not attend Zhou Yu’s funeral, though his death was mourned by Pang Tong.
	Zhou Yu’s death in the novel comes a little bit after his historical death.

full_text:
Zhou Yu (Gongjin) 周瑜 (公瑾)
ARomance of the Three KingdomsEncyclopedia officer profile.
Zhou Yu (周瑜), styledGongjin (公瑾), was born in Shu county, Lujiang commandery. Skilled war commander of Wu. Commanded Wu forces at the Battle of Chibi. Zhou Yu was affiliated with Yuan Shu and the Wu Kingdom. Zhou Yu lived during the Later Han Dynasty periods of Chinese history.
Return to theThree Kingdoms Encyclopediaor browse theEncyclopedia Directory.
Return toThree Kingdoms Encyclopedia

Fact vs Fiction
Differences Between Fact and Common Fiction
Zhou Yu did not attempt to have Zhuge Liang assassinated. The rivalry between Zhou Yu and Zhuge Liang was added to add to the allure of Zhuge Liang.
Was not visited by Jiang Gan during the battle of Chibi.
Zhuge Liang did not attend Zhou Yu’s funeral, though his death was mourned by Pang Tong.
Zhou Yu’s death in the novel comes a little bit after his historical death.
